family: Fries-, Photo-Fries, and Anionic Ortho-Fries Rearrangement
rationale: This is the classic textbook example of the Fries rearrangement, featuring the conversion of phenyl acetate to o-hydroxyacetophenone. It clearly demonstrates the migration of an acyl group from a phenolic oxygen to an ortho position on the aromatic ring, catalyzed by a Lewis acid (AlCl3). The reactants and products are simple, acyclic where possible, and unambiguously represent the core transformation of this reaction family.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses a simple phenyl group and an acetyl group, avoiding complex or highly functionalized arenes that could overlap with other named reactions involving aromatic systems. The core transformation is a specific intramolecular acyl migration, which is distinct from intermolecular acylations (like Friedel-Crafts acylation) or other rearrangements that do not involve a phenolic ester starting material.
